algorithms.title: Opioid-Associated Emergency Algorithm (BLS)
algorithms.description: The 2025 approach to a suspected opioid-associated life-threatening emergency for a BLS provider or lay rescuer.
steps: Suspect an opioid emergency in an unresponsive person with slow/absent breathing, pinpoint pupils, or paraphernalia.
Pulse present but not breathing normally: open the airway, give breaths (adult 1 every 6 seconds), and administer naloxone (intranasal or IM).
No pulse: begin high-quality CPR and apply an AED immediately — do not delay compressions for naloxone; give naloxone as an adjunct once CPR is underway.
Reassess; if breathing remains inadequate, continue ventilation and repeat naloxone (for example, about every 4 minutes).
After improvement, monitor closely for re-narcotization, keep the person under observation, and arrange transport and medical evaluation.
